label: Reservoir computing (ESN / LSM)

node.label: Reservoir computing (ESN / LSM)
node.definition: Reservoir computing drives a fixed recurrent dynamical system with input and trains only a lightweight readout from its rich transient state.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Reservoir computing (ESN / LSM) is modeled as depending on Recurrence & feedback state, Dynamical systems & control theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Neuromorphic, sensor & edge intelligence.
examples: atlas downstream link to Neuromorphic, sensor & edge intelligence
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Recurrence & feedback state, Dynamical systems & control theory.

dependencies.rationale: Reservoir computing (ESN / LSM) is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Recurrence & feedback state.
Reservoir computing (ESN / LSM) rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Dynamical systems & control theory.
Reservoir computing (ESN / LSM) follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
